one_time_description: Impairment loss on investment in DS-LBB Holdings Inc. (50% interest) due to difference between share of net assets and carrying value
economic_notes: First Nation has investment in Rapid Dose Therapeutics Inc. (publicly traded, formerly Consolidated Craft Brands via amalgamation); 50% interest in DS-LBB Holdings Inc.; wholly owns Day Star Gas Bar & Confectionary Inc.; member of Touchwood Agency Tribal Council; COVID-19 impacted operations during fiscal year
notes: Fiscal year is 2021 not 2024. Consolidated entities include Day Star First Nation CMHC Housing Projects and Day Star Resources Ltd./Day Star Resources Limited Partnership. GBEs accounted for by modified equity method: Day Star Gas Bar & Confectionary Inc. and DS-LBB Holdings Inc. (50%). Large impairment loss of $333,547 on DS-LBB Holdings Inc. investment recorded. Portfolio investment switched from Consolidated Craft Brands to Rapid Dose Therapeutics Inc. via amalgamation. Unrestricted deficit of $939,114 at year end. CMHC replacement reserve under-funded by $183,048 and operating reserve under-funded by $184,175. Touchwood Agency Band Misadministration Claim Trust contingency exists (subsequent event). ISC total_government_transfers estimated as ISC + FNIHB + CMHC + TATC = 3098064+653031+101603+609056=4461754; osr = 5241016-4461754=779262 adjusted to exclude loss from investments. Revenue lines include a net loss line from investments reducing total revenue.
warnings: One-time items ($1) represent 0.0% of revenue — surplus may not reflect ongoing operations
